scope: batch-add 6 easy default services to gmd-app source-build PoC; no contract flip, no routing, no chart change, no hard-runtime services

phase_a_script_extension.service_dispatch_strategy: explicit per-service entries in SERVICE_DOCKERFILE / SERVICE_CONTEXT associative arrays; no wildcard

tests.updated_existing_test: test_gmd_app_build_supported_services_tuple now pins all nine entries in dispatch order

commits_landed_to_canonical_main.summary: feat(gmd-app): batch-add 6 easy default services to source-build PoC

what_remains_before_chart_integration_li.summary: Only adservice and cartservice are left as default blockers. Once those land in the dispatch table, the dispatcher already has all 11 chart-default first-party services available.

for_remaining_two_services.adservice: Java toolchain (Gradle/JDK distroless). Adds a new substrate base set (eclipse-temurin or equivalent JDK + JRE) to whatever cluster-builder mirror is chosen.
for_remaining_two_services.cartservice: .NET 10 toolchain with NESTED context `src/cartservice/src`. Requires either a per-service `context_relpath` override (already supported by the SERVICE_CONTEXT map) or skaffold-parity dockerfile path. The mirror set is `mcr.microsoft.com/dotnet/sdk:10.0.100-noble` and `mcr.microsoft.com/dotnet/runtime-deps:10.0.0-noble-chiseled`.

for_chart_integration_minimal.approach: Recommended Option B from UP8-source-boundary audit: add helm.parameters to the dev element of infrastructure/gitops/gmd/applicationset.yaml flipping `images.repository` -> `host.k3d.internal:5000/gmd` and `images.tag` -> the gmd-app source-build tag for `gmd-dev` only.
for_chart_integration_minimal.blocked_until: all 11 default first-party services build successfully on the chosen substrate. After UP8-17, 9/11 are covered.

for_lifecycle_dispatch_wiring.change_required: scripts/amof/api/routers/release.py release_lifecycle_action() must branch on name == 'gmd' so action == 'build' calls build_gmd_app_lifecycle_build_command instead of build_lifecycle_build_command. Ideally with an iterator over the dispatch table for the full chart-default set.
for_lifecycle_dispatch_wiring.blocked_until: the command can produce the full set of chart-default gmd-deployable images, otherwise dispatched builds would always be incomplete

for_contract_flip.change_required: _ecosystem_build_contract('gmd') -> source_build with produces / source_repos / build_command metadata; update the lifecycle contract test that pins kind == 'mirror_upstream'
for_contract_flip.blocked_until: lifecycle dispatch wiring is in place AND the chart actually consumes the locally-built images

cluster_builder_substrate_decision.open_question: Does the gmd lifecycle build need to run inside the in-cluster builder pod (same path as AMOF platform substrate)?
cluster_builder_substrate_decision.if_yes_required_work_after_full_service_: Mirror per-language base images so the builder pod's containerd can pull them: golang:1.26.1-alpine, gcr.io/distroless/static, python:3.14.3-alpine, node:20.20.1-alpine, alpine:3.23.3, plus the Java base for adservice and the .NET bases for cartservice.
cluster_builder_substrate_decision.if_no: Host-only builds suffice for local development; production / cloud-dev substrate remains a separate decision.

what_to_implement: Add adservice (Java) to scripts/build-gmd-app.sh dispatch table.
Run host build for adservice and verify registry proof.
Still no contract flip, no routing, no chart change.
Add cartservice (.NET) to scripts/build-gmd-app.sh dispatch table with the nested context `src/cartservice/src` (already representable via the SERVICE_CONTEXT map).
Update _GMD_APP_BUILD_SUPPORTED_SERVICES and add focused test that pins the nested-context behavior.
Run host build for cartservice and verify registry proof.
Still no contract flip, no routing, no chart change.

next_slice_recommendation: Either; both are last-default-blockers. cartservice is preferred next because it exercises the nested-context branch of the dispatch table (already structurally supported via SERVICE_CONTEXT) before adservice introduces the largest new substrate footprint (Java).
